title: Ceraia philippinensis
taxonomy: Ceraia philippinensis is a species of moth in the family Sphingidae. It is a part of the genus Ceraia, which belongs to the order Lepidoptera. The classification of Ceraia philippinensis is further divided into its family, genus, and species levels. At the family level, it is classified as Sphingidae, which is a large family of moths known for their distinctive features. Ceraia philippinensis is also known to be a part of the order Lepidoptera, which includes butterflies and moths.

intro: Ceraia philippinensis, also known as Phil. philippinensis, is a species of orchid native to the Philippines. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ceraia philippinensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ceraia philippinensis is often prepared as a decoction or infused as a tea, and it is also used in the production of traditional remedies such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments.
